response.justification: Candidate is a seasoned senior engineer with broad full-stack experience and strong systems fundamentals (DLang at Sociomantic with low-latency async I/O, C++ at National Instruments, GIS work with GISCollective/Green Map). However, the role requires modern C++ or Rust as a primary skill, and the resume shows only early-career C/C++ exposure (~2000-2001) with no recent commercial C++ or any Rust experience. Geospatial background is a clear plus, but the core language gap and lack of recent routing/tiling or distributed pipeline work make this a borderline call.
strengths: Direct geospatial/GIS experience via GISCollective and Green Map
Low-latency, high-throughput systems background (Sociomantic/Dunnhumby, async I/O, fibers)
Strong testing/quality discipline (TDD, fluent-asserts, Trial test runner)
concerns: No recent modern C++ experience; C++ work is 25 years old
No demonstrated work on routing pipelines, tiling, or distributed map data systems
